name: Pot-Roasted Veal
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix 3 tablespoons olive oil, chopped shallots, half the ground rosemary, salt, and pepper together.
2. Rub this mixture onto all surfaces of the veal. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or overnight.
3. Heat the remaining olive oil in a Dutch oven or covered casserole over medium-high heat on top of the stove.
4. Add the veal and sear it on all sides until browned, about 15 minutes. Use large spoons or tongs to turn the meat without piercing it.
5. Drain any excess fat from the pan.
6. Add lemon juice, white wine, and the remaining rosemary to the pot. Cover and reduce heat to low.
7. Cook for 2 hours, or until the veal is tender. If the casserole becomes dry, add a tablespoon or two of water.
8. Turn off the heat and uncover the pot. Transfer the roast to a carving board and let it rest for 15 minutes before slicing.
9. Stir the butter into the cooking juices in the casserole.
10. To serve, cut the roast into 1/4-inch slices and arrange on a serving platter. Strain the sauce over the meat and serve immediately.
